mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above 5% for multiple consecutive months represents a restrictive policy stance that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing below-average S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days relative to periods of accommodative policy.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of t-1mo, near cycle highs
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS>5% periods are not statistically lower than the full-sample mean (t-test p>0.05), or rank IC of FEDFUNDS level with forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month decline in housing starts exceeding 10% signals deteriorating residential investment and forward-looking consumer demand, which historically precedes broader economic slowdown and depresses S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1408 thousand units, well below prior cycle peak near 2000+
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following a -10% 3-month HOUST decline are not significantly negative (p>0.05) or the effect reverses sign in more than 40% of triggered historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payroll growth decelerates below its 6-month trend while the unemployment rate simultaneously ticks up from cycle lows, the joint signal indicates a labor market inflection point that is not captured by either series alone, and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price a slowing consumption outlook.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth below 6-month average growth AND UNRATE rises >0.1pp from prior month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5%, near cycle lows as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Joint trigger instances show forward 20-day returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p>0.05), or the sign of the return is positive in more than 55% of triggered cases historically.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle tightening regime where FEDFUNDS has been elevated for over a year, rising CPIAUCSL inflation readings shift market expectations toward continued restrictive policy and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days, whereas in early-cycle or easing regimes the same CPI increase would not carry the same signal.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.153, FEDFUNDS at 5.26% indicating late-cycle restrictive stance
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI upside surprises in high-FEDFUNDS regimes are not significantly negative (p>0.05), or the relationship is equally strong in low-FEDFUNDS regimes, indicating no regime dependence.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects healthy real-economy supply-side momentum that supports corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.9282, consistent with continued expansion as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing INDPRO growth and forward 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following positive INDPRO growth are not higher than following negative growth (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in retail sales growth below its trailing 12-month average signals weakening consumer demand, which is the largest component of GDP, and leads to below-average S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days) as earnings expectations for consumer-facing sectors are revised down.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth falls below its trailing 12-month average by more than 1 standard deviation
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 370495 million dollars as of t-1mo
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after triggered RSAFS deceleration are not significantly lower than baseline (p>0.05), or fewer than 55% of historical trigger instances produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of declining housing starts and a restrictive federal funds rate jointly signals mounting credit and housing-market stress that individually each series understates, because high rates amplify the negative wealth and financing effects of a housing downturn, producing significantly negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1408, FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of March 2007 snapshot
regime_hint: housing downturn combined with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in joint-trigger periods are not significantly more negative than in periods where only one condition holds (p>0.05), indicating no genuine interaction effect beyond additive contributions.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by unemployment near multi-year lows (below 4.7%) alongside continued strong payroll growth, the labor market signal becomes a contrarian indicator: extremely tight labor conditions raise wage-inflation and rate-hike risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days, in contrast to early-cycle regimes where the same reading would be bullish.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% as of t-1mo, near multi-decade lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in low-UNRATE regimes are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p>0.05), or the sign of the relationship matches the early-cycle positive case, indicating no regime dependence.
